title: JV-18 - Release packaging for model bundle, ONNX or CT2 DLLs, licenses, checksums, and code-signing decision

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

body:
## Summary

Package the benchmark-approved local MT runtime and model into Windows release artifacts or document an external model install path.

## Why is this needed?

A default local MT release must actually ship or install the required model/runtime files safely.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Chosen runtime from JV-10/JV-11.
- Model manifest from JV-09.
- .github/workflows/release.yml.
- packaging/windows/tui-translator.iss.
- Code-signing and model-in-installer decision blockers.

## Outputs

- Release zip/installer packaging changes for runtime DLLs, model files or downloader, LICENSES, SHA256SUMS.
- Decision on bundling model weights vs first-run download.
- Code-signing certificate decision or explicit pre-release blocker.
- Artifact validation script.

## Implementation approach

- Bundle official runtime DLL next to exe when license permits.
- Include model license and attribution.
- Generate SHA256SUMS and optionally signatures.
- Installer preserves user model cache on uninstall unless user opts to remove data.
- Avoid mutable remote model URLs in release artifacts.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Release workflow publishes usable zip and installer for local MT path.
- [ ] Artifact validation verifies exe, DLL, model or manifest, licenses, docs, checksums.
- [ ] Installer installs and launches without VC++ runtime surprises.
- [ ] Code-signing status is explicit before stable release.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] GitHub Actions release dry-run or local package smoke.
- [ ] dumpbin/dependency check.
- [ ] Install/uninstall smoke on Windows.
- [ ] Checksum validation.
- [ ] Offline launch with bundled model or clear missing-model UX.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-09, JV-11, JV-13, JV-15.
- Blocks JV-19/JV-21.

## Agent routing

Release/packaging specialist. Security review for downloaded/bundled binaries and licenses.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-18
- Wave: RP1 packaging
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: release, area: packaging, area: providers, provider: local, priority:P0, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

Extends existing release.yml packaging; new model/DLL/code-signing scope.
